title: Wet-felted finger puppet set
subtitle: Three simple finger puppets in merino: a fox, a rabbit, and a hedgehog
excerpt: Wet-felt three simple finger puppets over a resist, each sized to fit an adult finger snugly. The fox, rabbit, and hedgehog forms are built from basic tube shapes with needle-felted face and character details added once dry.
sourceNotes: Synthesised from traditional wet-felting practice. Finger puppets felted over a resist are a classic beginner wet-felting project; the three British wildlife characters here follow standard animal-form conventions.

glossaryTerms.definition: A felting method using hot soapy water and agitation to lock wool fibres together into a firm fabric.
A waterproof template placed inside a wet-felt piece to create a hollow form. The felt forms around the resist and is removed once the piece has consolidated.

content.text: Each finger puppet is made by wet-felting around a
cut from thick plastic sheeting or laminated card. The felted tube shrinks onto the resist; once dry and removed, the hollow form slides over a finger.
gives the puppet a firm, smooth base; needle-felted details added after drying give each character its personality.

attrs.title: Resist size
attrs.body: Cut the resist about 20 percent larger than the desired finished puppet size to allow for shrinkage during felting.

items.name: Thick plastic sheeting for resist templates

content.text: Cut three resist templates from thick plastic sheeting, each a finger-width oval about 8 cm long and 3 cm wide. Round the top of each resist and leave the bottom open so the finger can enter.
Step 2: Lay and wet the roving

For each puppet, lay a thin layer of roving over the resist on both sides, extending about 1 cm beyond each edge. Wet with hot soapy water and press flat. Add a second layer at 90 degrees to the first. Fold the overhanging edges over onto the back of the resist.
Step 3: Felt and full each puppet

Rub each puppet with a soapy palm for 5 to 8 minutes, turning frequently. The felt should firm up and hold together. Full by rolling each puppet under your palm on the mat, then rinse in cool water. Slide the resist out while the puppet is still damp. Allow to dry.

Once the puppets are dry, add needle-felted character details. For the fox: add orange ear triangles with white inner ear, a white muzzle, and a black nose. For the rabbit: add tall white oval ears with pale pink inner and a small pink nose. For the hedgehog: jab dark brown roving over the back half to create spiky texture, then add a pale snout.

items.problem: The puppet is too small to fit over a finger
items.cause: The resist was too small or too much shrinkage occurred
items.fix: Stretch the puppet over a finger-sized wooden or cork dowel while still damp and allow it to dry on the form.

items.problem: Holes appeared at the top of the puppet
items.cause: The roving was not laid thickly enough at the curved top of the resist
items.fix: Patch the hole with a small wisp of roving and wet-felt it in by rubbing with a damp soapy palm.
